query: Compare diabetes prevalence between Cook County, IL and Lake County, IL in 2022. Which has higher prevalence?
responses: Agent should retrieve diabetes data for both counties including prevalence and confidence intervals. Should note that both counties have identical point estimates (11.9%) and broadly overlapping confidence intervals. Should conclude that there is no meaningful difference in diabetes prevalence between these counties. May note that Cook County has a slightly narrower CI due to larger population size.

query: Compare obesity prevalence between Census Tract 21111010701 and Census Tract 21111003000 in Jefferson County, Kentucky in 2020. Which tract has lower obesity?
responses: Agent should retrieve obesity data for both census tracts including prevalence and confidence intervals. Should note that only crude prevalence estimates are available for census tracts (age-adjusted not available at this level). Should compare both point estimates AND confidence intervals, noting they do not overlap. Should conclude that Census Tract 21111010701 has significantly lower obesity (26.9%) compared to Census Tract 21111003000 (57.0%). This is a valid comparison with narrow, non-overlapping CIs showing a meaningful 30+ percentage point difference.

query: Compare smoking rates between Austin, TX and San Antonio, TX in 2021. Can we conclude which city has higher smoking rates?
responses: Agent should retrieve age-adjusted smoking prevalence data for both cities (since age-adjusted estimates are available and recommended for place-level comparisons). Should note Austin has 11.2% (95% CI: 9.8-12.7%) and San Antonio has 14.6% (95% CI: 12.6-16.7%). Should confirm this is a valid comparison with non-overlapping confidence intervals, and conclude that San Antonio has significantly higher smoking rates. May also note the 3.4 percentage point difference and that both cities' crude rates are similar to their age-adjusted rates, suggesting similar age distributions.

query: Compare stroke prevalence between ZCTA 10002 (Lower East Side, Manhattan) and ZCTA 13339 (rural NY) in 2023. Which area has higher stroke prevalence?
responses: Agent should retrieve stroke prevalence data for both ZCTAs including confidence intervals and note population sizes. Should find both have 4.1% point estimates but very different populations (10002: ~82,000; 13339: ~6,600). Should compare confidence intervals and note whether they overlap. The correct conclusion is that there is no meaningful difference in stroke prevalence between these areas - both have 4.1% point estimates. May note that the urban ZCTA likely has a narrower CI due to much larger population, but the key finding is that a rural area and urban neighborhood can have equivalent stroke rates.

query: Compare asthma prevalence between Sumter County, FL (predominantly retirement community) and Centre County, PA (college town with Penn State University) in 2019. Retrieve both crude and age-adjusted rates. Which county has higher asthma prevalence and which estimate type is more appropriate for comparison?
responses: Agent should retrieve BOTH crude and age-adjusted asthma prevalence for both counties. Should recognize the extreme age distribution differences between a retirement community and college town. Should present both crude rates (Sumter: 7.5% CI: 7.0-8.1%; Centre: 10.1% CI: 9.5-10.8%) and age-adjusted rates (Sumter: 7.9% CI: 7.5-8.3%; Centre: 9.9% CI: 9.4-10.4%), then emphasize that age-adjusted estimates are the appropriate comparison for county-level data per CDC guidance. Should conclude Centre County has higher asthma prevalence (9.9% vs 7.9%) based on age-adjusted rates with non-overlapping CIs. Should explain that the age-adjusted comparison controls for the dramatic age differences between these populations, making it the valid basis for comparison.

query: Compare high blood pressure prevalence between Miami-Dade County (crude) and Collier County (age-adjusted) in Florida for 2023. Which county has higher prevalence?
responses: Agent should retrieve high blood pressure data for both counties. Will find Miami-Dade has age-adjusted rate of 30.3% (95% CI: 27.2-33.6%) while Collier has crude rate of 39.2% (95% CI: 35.4-43.0%). Agent should recognize these are DIFFERENT estimate types and state that direct comparison is NOT valid. Must explain that you cannot compare age-adjusted (Miami-Dade) to crude (Collier) estimates - both counties must use the same estimate type. Should recommend retrieving the matching estimate types (either both age-adjusted or both crude) before making any comparison. Should NOT conclude which county has higher prevalence without proper comparable data.

query: Compare obesity rates between Census Tract 29189216300 and Census Tract 29189213900 in St. Louis County, Missouri in 2020 using age-adjusted values.
responses: Agent should immediately recognize that age-adjusted estimates are NOT available at the census tract level per CDC PLACES methodology and explain this upfront. Should state that only crude prevalence estimates are available for census tracts (age-adjusted estimates are only provided for counties and places). Should then retrieve and compare crude estimates: Census Tract 29189216300 has 18.3% obesity (95% CI: 16.8-19.5%) and Census Tract 29189213900 has 47.6% obesity (95% CI: 46.2-48.8%). Should note non-overlapping CIs support valid comparison showing Census Tract 29189213900 has significantly higher obesity (47.6% vs 18.3%). Should explain that age-adjusted estimates are unavailable at tract level due to small sample sizes and the statistical challenges of age-standardization for small areas. The agent should NOT attempt to retrieve age-adjusted data for census tracts.

query: Has diabetes prevalence increased in Austin, TX between 2019 and 2023? Compare the diabetes rates for these two years.
responses: Agent may retrieve the diabetes data showing 2019: 7.4% (95% CI: 7.4-7.5%) and 2023: 8.1% (95% CI: 7.2-9.0%), but should immediately recognize that temporal comparisons within the same geographic area are NOT valid for CDC PLACES data. Should explain that the current modeling procedure does not support tracking changes at the local level over time because: (1) Place, census tract, and ZCTA estimates use 2010/2020 U.S. Census as the post-stratification dataset and cannot incorporate year-to-year population changes; (2) The model assumes the population in any given year is the same as measured in 2010/2020; (3) Time is not included as a variable in the model. Should state that while the data exists for both years, these estimates cannot be validly compared to assess whether diabetes actually increased. The agent should NOT conclude whether diabetes increased, decreased, or remained stable. May note that Austin's population increased significantly (806,804 to 961,855) but this real population change is not reflected in the modeling. This limitation applies to all place, tract, and ZCTA level comparisons over time, and even county-level estimates cannot be used to assess trends since time is not a model variable.
